figures.caption: Table 1. Generative metrics as measured by FID, precision (P), recall (R), density (D), coverage (C), and MS-SSIM.

Fig. 1. Overview of the VolDiT framework. Input volumes are encoded into latents, patchified into tokens, and processed by the 3D Diffusion Transformer for denoising, with spatial conditioning injected through the timestep-gated control adapter. The transformer outputs are then unpatchified and decoded to reconstruct volumetric images.

Fig. 2. Synthetic examples on LUNA16 and TaviCT. HA-GAN produces anatomically implausible structures, while the U-Net LDM yields high-quality images but struggles with global anatomical consistency (red circles). VolDiT generates anatomically coherent volumes with sharper structural detail, reflecting the benefit of global self-attention.
